scenarios.name: conservative
scenarios.thesis: Earnings is a binary catalyst with options pricing a +/-10.5% move and fundamentals requiring a >+15% EPS surprise just to drive upside (T+5 +18%, 80% hit). With portfolio empty and chart data unavailable, the asymmetry does not justify pre-print risk; wait for post-print clarity.

actions.name: Palantir Technologies
actions.rationale: Watch-only into 2026-05-04 print. Re-evaluate on 2026-05-05 once the surprise bucket is known: only act if reaction lands in the >+15% beat bucket (avg T+5 +18%) and price stabilizes, or if a miss-driven -10% to -15% drawdown brings valuation closer to peer median 18x fwd PE (vs current 109x).

scenarios.name: base
scenarios.thesis: Take a small starter pre-print to participate if the >+15% surprise bucket triggers (T+5 +18%, 80% hit), and keep dry powder to add only after the print resolves the binary. Defensive setup (-22% YTD, software derated) limits downside vs cohort, but 109x fwd PE caps upside on anything less than a blowout.

actions.name: Palantir Technologies
actions.rationale: Starter sized at 2% of portfolio (well below 10% concentration cap) sized down because reaction-bucket math is unfavorable in the modal 5-15% beat outcome (T+5 +1%, non-event). Confidence-weighted: fundamental confidence 0.50, chart confidence 0.0 -> half of a normal 4% starter.

actions.name: Palantir Technologies
actions.rationale: Conditional add executed only on 2026-05-05 if EPS surprise lands in the >+15% blowout bucket (historical T+5 +18%, 80% hit). Skip the add on 5-15% beat (non-event) or miss. Brings total PLTR weight to 5% — within moderate-risk single-name budget.

scenarios.name: aggressive
scenarios.thesis: Lean into the defensive setup (already -22% YTD, software cohort derated, options ±10.5% is below historical blowout move) and pre-position larger ahead of print, then double down on a >+15% surprise. Accept the valuation tail risk (109x fwd PE) for asymmetric upside if AIP commercial traction or government acceleration drives a top-line beat.

actions.name: Palantir Technologies
actions.rationale: Pre-print position sized to 6% (under 15% aggressive cap). Justified by defensive technical positioning per fundamental report and 56% rev growth + 46% adj op margin + $2.1B FCF + zero LT debt fundamentals. Sized to survive miss-bucket -15% drawdown (~0.9% portfolio loss).

actions.name: Palantir Technologies
actions.rationale: Post-print add only if surprise >+15% (blowout bucket, T+5 +18% / 80% hit). Total PLTR weight then 10%, still under aggressive 15% single-name cap. Skip if 5-15% beat (non-event bucket) or miss.

open_questions: Asymmetric setup tension: defensive positioning (-22% YTD, software derated, options ±10.5% reasonable) argues FOR pre-print exposure, but 109x fwd PE vs peer median 18x is the binding constraint and means even a 5-15% beat is a non-event (T+5 +1%). The only clearly profitable bucket is >+15% surprise.

Chart data is unavailable (yfinance blocked, ChartReport confidence 0.0). Stop_loss and target levels intentionally left null — DO NOT fabricate technical levels. Re-run chart-analyst once data access is restored before placing any limit/stop orders.
Fundamental confidence is only 0.50 (web-sourced, not live API). Validate next-quarter guidance language and AIP commercial revenue mix on the 2026-05-04 call before executing the conditional add.
Would deviate from base toward conservative if: (a) PLTR rallies >+5% into the print (compresses asymmetry further), or (b) software cohort re-rates higher pre-print removing the defensive cushion.

Would deviate from base toward aggressive if: (a) PLTR drops another -10% into the print on no company-specific news, or (b) chart data becomes available and confirms a clean technical base near support.

Portfolio is hypothetical 100M KRW with zero existing holdings — no concentration, sector, or overlap constraints binding. Real allocation should re-check against actual holdings once portfolio.yaml is populated.
FX: PLTR trades USD in us-main account. KRW base currency means an additional USD/KRW risk layer on top of equity risk; not modeled here.
